title: The Brain That Changes Itself
author: Norman Doidge
core_idea: The brain is not a fixed organ but a dynamic, adaptable system capable of rewiring itself throughout life. Doidge presents compelling case studies demonstrating neuroplasticity — the brain's ability to reorganize and heal.
key_concepts: Neuroplasticity: the brain can change its structure and function based on experience.
Mental and physical exercises can reshape neural pathways.
The brain can compensate for damage by recruiting other areas.
Repetition and focused attention drive neural change.
Negative plasticity can reinforce bad habits just as easily as positive ones.
notable_quote: The brain is a far more open system than we ever imagined, and nature has gone very far to help us perceive and take in the world around us.
practical_lessons: Deliberate practice can literally rewire your brain.
Age does not prevent the brain from changing — use it or lose it applies at any age.
Visualization and mental rehearsal can produce real physical changes in the brain.
Breaking bad habits requires replacing them with new, repetitive positive behaviors.
